rings.name: ADOPT
rings.description: Proven, production-ready technologies that Red Hat and IBM actively recommend and ship.

rings.name: TRIAL
rings.description: Technologies gaining traction and recommended for broader adoption with appropriate evaluation.

rings.name: ASSESS
rings.description: Emerging technologies worth exploring, but not yet ready for production at scale.

rings.name: HOLD
rings.description: Technologies that are deprecated, end-of-life, or superseded — avoid for new projects.

entries.title: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL)
entries.description: The world's leading enterprise Linux platform. Foundation for Red Hat's entire portfolio and the primary OS for hybrid cloud workloads.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform 4
entries.description: Enterprise Kubernetes platform with built-in operations, security, and developer tooling. The cornerstone of Red Hat's hybrid cloud strategy.

entries.title: CRI-O
entries.description: Lightweight OCI-compliant container runtime purpose-built for Kubernetes. Default container runtime in OpenShift 4.

entries.title: KVM Hypervisor
entries.description: Kernel-based Virtual Machine — the Linux hypervisor at the core of Red Hat Virtualization, OpenStack, and OpenShift Virtualization workloads.

entries.title: Fedora CoreOS
entries.description: Immutable, automatically-updating OS for running containerised workloads. Community upstream for RHEL CoreOS (the OCP node OS).

entries.title: CentOS Stream
entries.description: Continuously-delivered distribution that tracks just ahead of the next RHEL release. The official upstream for RHEL minor releases.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift Service on AWS (ROSA)
entries.description: Fully-managed OpenShift service on AWS, jointly supported by Red Hat and AWS. Enables cloud-native development without cluster administration.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift Dedicated
entries.description: Red Hat-managed OpenShift clusters on AWS or GCP. Removes operational burden while keeping full enterprise Kubernetes capabilities.

entries.title: IBM Cloud
entries.description: IBM's public cloud platform spanning 60+ data centres. Offers IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS with strong AI, security, and hybrid cloud capabilities.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift on IBM Cloud
entries.description: Managed OpenShift service on IBM Cloud. Combines OpenShift's developer experience with IBM Cloud's compliance and AI capabilities.

entries.title: IBM Z (Mainframe)
entries.description: IBM's mainframe platform (z16, z17) with on-chip AI via Telum processors. Handles billions of transactions daily with industry-leading reliability.

entries.title: IBM Power Systems
entries.description: High-performance RISC servers (Power10, Power11) for AI inference, ERP, and cloud workloads. Primary platform for AIX and IBM i.

entries.title: Fedora Linux
entries.description: Community Linux distribution sponsored by Red Hat. The cutting-edge upstream from which RHEL is derived.

entries.title: Red Hat Enterprise Linux for Edge
entries.description: RHEL tailored for edge deployments — minimal footprint, image-based updates, and support for disconnected environments.

entries.title: IBM FlashSystem
entries.description: High-performance all-flash SAN/NAS storage arrays for mission-critical workloads and AI data pipelines.

entries.title: IBM Storage Scale (Spectrum Scale)
entries.description: Parallel clustered file system for HPC, AI training, and big data analytics at petabyte scale.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift Virtualization
entries.description: Run and manage virtual machines alongside containers on OpenShift, powered by KubeVirt. The strategic replacement for Red Hat Virtualization.

entries.title: MicroShift
entries.description: Lightweight OpenShift optimised for resource-constrained edge devices. Provides Kubernetes API compatibility with a tiny footprint.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift Serverless
entries.description: Knative-based serverless platform on OpenShift. Supports event-driven and scale-to-zero workloads.

entries.title: IBM Cloud Code Engine
entries.description: Fully-managed serverless platform for running containers, batch jobs, and functions on IBM Cloud without managing infrastructure.

entries.title: Red Hat Advanced Cluster Management (ACM)
entries.description: Multi-cluster Kubernetes management with policy-based governance, application lifecycle, and observability across hybrid cloud fleets.

entries.title: Red Hat Device Edge
entries.description: Combination of RHEL for Edge and MicroShift packaged for industrial and field edge deployments.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenStack Platform
entries.description: Red Hat's distribution of OpenStack for private cloud IaaS. Mainstream investment is shifting toward OpenShift Virtualization for new workloads.

timeline.description: Strategic focus moving to OpenShift Virtualization

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift 3.x
entries.description: First-generation OpenShift based on Docker and Ansible. Extended Life Support ended June 2024 — migrate to OCP 4.

entries.title: OpenShift Online
entries.description: Red Hat's hosted OpenShift SaaS offering. Shut down September 2023; users migrated to ROSA or Developer Sandbox.

entries.title: CoreOS Container Linux
entries.description: Original immutable OS for containers from CoreOS Inc. EOL May 2020 after Red Hat acquisition; superseded by Fedora CoreOS.

entries.title: Red Hat Virtualization (RHV)
entries.description: oVirt-based enterprise VM platform. EOL August 2026; Red Hat recommends migrating to OpenShift Virtualization.

timeline.description: EOL announced; migration path to OpenShift Virtualization

entries.title: oVirt
entries.description: Community upstream of Red Hat Virtualization. In maintenance mode as investment moves to KubeVirt / OpenShift Virtualization.

entries.title: Red Hat build of Quarkus
entries.description: Cloud-native Java framework optimised for Kubernetes. Supersonic startup times and low memory via GraalVM native compilation or JVM mode.

entries.title: Red Hat JBoss EAP
entries.description: Enterprise Java application server (Jakarta EE / MicroProfile). JBoss EAP 8 targets Java 17/21 and modern cloud deployments.

entries.title: Red Hat JBoss Web Server
entries.description: Supported distribution of Apache Tomcat and Apache HTTP Server for enterprise Java web applications.

entries.title: Open Liberty
entries.description: IBM's open-source Jakarta EE and MicroProfile runtime. Lightweight, composable feature set ideal for cloud-native Java microservices.

entries.title: Eclipse OpenJ9 / IBM Semeru Runtimes
entries.description: High-performance JVM with significantly lower memory footprint and fast startup. Packaged as IBM Semeru Runtimes (certified OpenJDK distributions).

entries.title: Red Hat build of OpenJDK
entries.description: Red Hat's supported OpenJDK distribution for RHEL, Quarkus, and JBoss middleware. Red Hat is a leading contributor to the OpenJDK project.

entries.title: Red Hat build of Node.js
entries.description: Supported Node.js runtime for RHEL and OpenShift. Used for server-side JavaScript applications and tooling across the Red Hat ecosystem.

entries.title: Eclipse Vert.x
entries.description: Reactive toolkit for building non-blocking, event-driven applications on the JVM. Core foundation of Quarkus's reactive stack.

entries.title: MicroProfile
entries.description: Community specification for cloud-native Java microservices. Supported by Red Hat (Quarkus) and IBM (Open Liberty, WebSphere Liberty).

entries.title: Red Hat build of Camel K
entries.description: Serverless integration runtime for running Apache Camel routes natively on Kubernetes, with minimal configuration.

entries.title: Knative
entries.description: Kubernetes-based platform for deploying and managing serverless workloads. Upstream of Red Hat OpenShift Serverless.

entries.title: GraalVM Native Image
entries.description: Ahead-of-time compiler turning JVM applications into fast-starting native binaries. Mandrel (Red Hat's distribution) targets Quarkus use cases.

entries.title: JBoss EAP 5 / 6
entries.description: Legacy JBoss application server versions based on older Java EE specifications. Long past EOL — migrate to JBoss EAP 8 or Quarkus.

entries.title: JBoss Portal
entries.description: Legacy Java EE portal server. Discontinued; replaced by modern SPA frameworks and developer portals such as RHDH.

entries.title: Red Hat Mobile Application Platform
entries.description: Mobile backend-as-a-service and app development platform. Discontinued; capabilities absorbed into broader OpenShift tooling.

entries.title: WebSphere Application Server (Classic)
entries.description: IBM's traditional monolithic Java EE app server. Still supported for legacy workloads, but Open Liberty is preferred for all new development.

entries.title: SwitchYard ESB
entries.description: Legacy lightweight ESB built on CDI. Replaced by Red Hat Fuse and subsequently by Red Hat build of Apache Camel.

entries.title: Red Hat Ansible Automation Platform
entries.description: Enterprise IT automation platform for configuration management, provisioning, and orchestration. Agentless, YAML-based, and widely adopted across hybrid cloud.

entries.title: Podman
entries.description: Daemonless, rootless container engine with a Docker-compatible CLI. Default container tool in RHEL and Fedora.

entries.title: Buildah
entries.description: OCI-compliant container image builder. Builds images without a daemon, enabling secure CI/CD pipelines and rootless builds.

entries.title: Skopeo
entries.description: Command-line tool for inspecting, copying, and signing container images across registries without a local daemon.

entries.title: Helm
entries.description: The Kubernetes package manager. First-class support in OpenShift via the OperatorHub and Developer Catalog.

entries.title: Operator Framework / OLM
entries.description: SDK and lifecycle manager (OLM) for building and running Kubernetes Operators. Originated at Red Hat and donated to CNCF.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift Pipelines
entries.description: Tekton-based cloud-native CI/CD for Kubernetes. Pipelines run as isolated containers with no shared state.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift GitOps
entries.description: Argo CD-based GitOps operator for OpenShift. Ensures cluster state matches what is declared in Git.

entries.title: Red Hat Service Mesh
entries.description: Istio-based service mesh for OpenShift. Provides traffic management, mTLS, observability, and policy for microservices.

entries.title: Red Hat build of Keycloak
entries.description: Enterprise identity and access management. Supports OIDC, SAML, and social login. Successor to Red Hat Single Sign-On.

entries.title: Red Hat 3scale API Management
entries.description: Full lifecycle API management platform. Controls access, enforces policies, and provides developer portals for API consumers.

entries.title: Red Hat Satellite
entries.description: Lifecycle and patch management for RHEL fleets. Provides content management, subscription tracking, and remote execution at scale.

entries.title: Red Hat Advanced Cluster Security (ACS)
entries.description: Kubernetes-native security platform (StackRox acquisition). Provides vulnerability management, policy enforcement, and runtime threat detection.

timeline.description: StackRox acquisition integrated into Red Hat portfolio

entries.title: IBM Turbonomic
entries.description: AI-powered application resource management. Continuously optimises performance and cost across hybrid cloud by making autonomous workload placement decisions.

entries.title: IBM Instana Observability
entries.description: Automated APM and observability with sub-second granularity and AI-driven root-cause analysis. Supports containerised and microservice architectures.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift Local
entries.description: Run a full OpenShift cluster on a developer laptop. Formerly CodeReady Containers (CRC). Essential for local OpenShift development and testing.

entries.title: IBM API Connect
entries.description: Full-lifecycle API management with gateway, developer portal, and analytics. Supports REST, GraphQL, and event-driven APIs.

entries.title: Red Hat Developer Hub
entries.description: Enterprise internal developer portal built on Backstage. Provides software catalogs, TechDocs, golden-path templates, and plugin ecosystem.

timeline.description: GA announced at Red Hat Summit 2023

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift Dev Spaces
entries.description: Browser-based cloud development environments running on OpenShift. Successor to CodeReady Workspaces; based on Eclipse Che.

entries.title: Podman Desktop
entries.description: Open-source GUI for managing containers and Kubernetes with Podman. Cross-platform alternative to Docker Desktop.

entries.title: Ansible Lightspeed
entries.description: AI-powered Ansible content assistant using IBM watsonx. Generates playbook tasks from natural language prompts inside VS Code.

entries.title: Pipelines as Code
entries.description: Define Tekton CI/CD pipelines in-repository as YAML, triggered by pull requests and pushes. Part of OpenShift Pipelines.

entries.title: watsonx Code Assistant
entries.description: IBM's AI code generation and explanation tool, powered by Granite models. Supports Java modernisation, Ansible, and Z/COBOL use cases.

entries.title: IBM watsonx Orchestrate
entries.description: AI-powered automation assistant that assembles multi-step business workflows from skills and API integrations.

entries.title: IBM Event Endpoint Management
entries.description: Unified catalog for discovering and managing Kafka topics and REST APIs as governed event endpoints.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift Lightspeed
entries.description: Generative AI assistant embedded in the OpenShift web console. Answers operational questions and helps with troubleshooting in natural language.

entries.title: InstructLab
entries.description: Open-source tooling for aligning and fine-tuning LLMs using a taxonomy-driven data synthesis approach. Co-developed by Red Hat and IBM.

entries.title: Source-to-Image (S2I)
entries.description: Builds reproducible container images directly from source code on OpenShift. Useful for brownfield apps; newer projects prefer Tekton and Dockerfiles.

entries.title: Rook
entries.description: Cloud-native storage orchestrator for Kubernetes, primarily used to deploy and manage Ceph clusters. Underpins OpenShift Data Foundation.

entries.title: IBM Guardium AI Security
entries.description: Early-stage security controls for discovering, monitoring, and protecting AI models and training data from risks and compliance violations.

entries.title: Minishift
entries.description: Local OpenShift 3 environment via miniaturised VM. Unmaintained since OpenShift 4 launch; replaced by Red Hat OpenShift Local (CRC).

entries.title: Red Hat Container Development Kit (CDK)
entries.description: Pre-configured development environment for OpenShift 3. Superseded by Red Hat OpenShift Local (formerly CodeReady Containers).

entries.title: CodeReady Workspaces
entries.description: Browser-based IDE on OpenShift based on Eclipse Che. Rebranded and superseded by Red Hat OpenShift Dev Spaces.

entries.title: Red Hat CloudForms
entries.description: Hybrid cloud management platform. EOL March 2023; capabilities moved into Ansible Automation Platform and RHACM.

entries.title: ManageIQ
entries.description: Open-source upstream of Red Hat CloudForms. Community-maintained; investment has moved on.

entries.title: Red Hat Developer Studio
entries.description: Eclipse-based IDE bundled with Red Hat middleware tooling. Discontinued; developers now use VS Code with Red Hat extensions or OpenShift Dev Spaces.

entries.title: IBM Rational ClearCase
entries.description: Legacy IBM version control system. Moving to limited support on many platforms — migrate to Git-based workflows.

entries.title: IBM Connections
entries.description: Social collaboration platform for the enterprise. Limited ongoing IBM investment; many organisations have moved to Microsoft 365 or Slack.

entries.title: Red Hat Single Sign-On (SSO)
entries.description: Earlier Red Hat branding for its Keycloak-based IAM product. Replaced by the 'Red Hat build of Keycloak' from version 22 onwards.

timeline.description: Replaced by Red Hat build of Keycloak

entries.title: Red Hat AMQ Streams (Apache Kafka)
entries.description: Enterprise Apache Kafka distribution for OpenShift and RHEL. Strimzi-based Operator provides lifecycle management, rebalancing, and mirror-maker.

entries.title: Red Hat AMQ Broker
entries.description: High-performance messaging broker based on Apache ActiveMQ Artemis. Supports JMS, AMQP, MQTT, and STOMP protocols.

entries.title: Red Hat Ceph Storage
entries.description: Unified block, file, and object storage built on Ceph. Designed for cloud-scale deployments and underpins OpenShift Data Foundation.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift Data Foundation
entries.description: Software-defined storage for OpenShift, built on Ceph and NooBaa. Provides persistent block, file, and object storage for stateful applications.

entries.title: Red Hat build of Apache Camel
entries.description: Integration framework with 300+ connectors for enterprise integration patterns. The strategic replacement for Red Hat Fuse.

entries.title: IBM MQ
entries.description: Enterprise message queue for guaranteed, once-and-only-once delivery. Industry standard for financial services and transactional integration.

entries.title: IBM App Connect Enterprise
entries.description: Integration platform with AI-powered mapping, low-code flows, and 200+ pre-built connectors. Formerly IBM Integration Bus.

entries.title: IBM Event Streams
entries.description: Managed Apache Kafka service on IBM Cloud and Cloud Pak for Integration. Provides event streaming for real-time data pipelines.

entries.title: IBM Db2
entries.description: IBM's flagship relational database for OLTP and analytics. Decades of enterprise adoption with deep Z and Power integration.

entries.title: IBM Cognos Analytics
entries.description: Business intelligence and reporting platform with AI-driven data preparation, dashboards, and natural language query.

entries.title: IBM Cloudant
entries.description: Fully managed NoSQL document database optimised for mobile, web, and IoT applications with a CouchDB-compatible API.

entries.title: IBM watsonx.ai
entries.description: AI studio for training, validating, tuning, and deploying foundation models. Hosts IBM Granite and supports third-party models.

entries.title: IBM watsonx.data
entries.description: Open data lakehouse built on Presto and Apache Iceberg. Designed for cost-effective AI data preparation and analytics across hybrid cloud.

entries.title: IBM watsonx.governance
entries.description: AI governance, risk, and compliance platform. Monitors models for drift, bias, and regulatory compliance.

entries.title: IBM watsonx Assistant
entries.description: Enterprise conversational AI platform with RAG, GenAI answer generation, and multi-channel deployment capabilities.

entries.title: IBM QRadar SIEM
entries.description: Security information and event management platform for threat detection, correlation, and compliance reporting.

entries.title: IBM QRadar SOAR
entries.description: Security orchestration, automation, and response platform for incident response workflows and playbook execution.

entries.title: IBM Guardium Data Security
entries.description: Data discovery, classification, activity monitoring, and compliance automation for structured and unstructured data at rest and in motion.

entries.title: IBM Cloud Pak for Data
entries.description: Unified data and AI platform on OpenShift. Integrates data fabric, governance, analytics, and AI lifecycle in one system.

entries.title: IBM SPSS Statistics
entries.description: Statistical analysis software for survey analysis, hypothesis testing, and predictive analytics. Standard tool in research and social science.

entries.title: IBM Netezza Performance Server
entries.description: Cloud-native data warehouse with massively parallel processing and a watsonx-powered SQL assistant for analytics at scale.

entries.title: IBM Sterling B2B Integrator
entries.description: Enterprise B2B integration for EDI, managed file transfer, and partner connectivity. Widely used in supply chain and logistics.

entries.title: Hyperledger Fabric
entries.description: Permissioned enterprise blockchain framework for business networks. IBM was a founding contributor and primary driver.

entries.title: IBM Verify (Trusteer)
entries.description: Fraud detection and risk-based authentication for digital channels. Includes device fingerprinting, malware detection, and adaptive access.

entries.title: Red Hat OpenShift AI
entries.description: Data science and MLOps platform on OpenShift. Provides managed Jupyter notebooks, model serving, pipelines, and GPU resource management.

entries.title: NooBaa
entries.description: Multicloud object data service providing a single namespace across S3-compatible stores. Core of the OpenShift Data Foundation object gateway.

entries.title: IBM Granite Models
entries.description: Family of open-source foundation models (code, language, time series) developed by IBM Research. Available on watsonx.ai and Hugging Face.

entries.title: IBM Qiskit / Quantum Runtime
entries.description: Open-source SDK and server-side execution framework for IBM Quantum computers. Enables hybrid quantum-classical workloads.

entries.title: IBM SPSS Modeler
entries.description: Visual predictive analytics and machine learning modelling tool. Used for churn prediction, fraud detection, and risk scoring.

entries.title: IBM Cloud Pak for Integration
entries.description: Unified integration platform on OpenShift bundling MQ, App Connect, API Connect, Event Streams, and DataPower Gateway.

entries.title: Red Hat Enterprise Linux AI
entries.description: Foundation model image built on RHEL for running IBM Granite models locally with hardware acceleration on bare metal or VMs.

entries.title: Red Hat AI Inference Server
entries.description: vLLM-based inference server optimised for running large language models at scale across hybrid cloud and on-premises deployments.

entries.title: Neural Magic (Red Hat)
entries.description: AI model optimisation and sparse inference acquired by Red Hat in 2024. Enables LLM deployment on CPU and GPU without performance compromise.

entries.title: IBM Quantum Systems
entries.description: IBM's gate-based quantum computers (Heron, Condor processors) available via IBM Cloud. Roadmap targets fault-tolerant quantum advantage.

entries.title: Red Hat Gluster Storage
entries.description: Scale-out network-attached storage based on GlusterFS. EOL December 2024; Red Hat recommends migrating to Ceph / OpenShift Data Foundation.

entries.title: Red Hat Fuse 7
entries.description: Camel-based integration platform. EOL June 2024; migrate to Red Hat build of Apache Camel or Camel K.

timeline.description: EOL; replaced by Red Hat build of Apache Camel

entries.title: Red Hat JBoss A-MQ 6
entries.description: ActiveMQ 5-based message broker. EOL February 2025; upgrade to Red Hat AMQ Broker 7 (ActiveMQ Artemis).

entries.title: HornetQ
entries.description: Legacy Red Hat messaging system that evolved into ActiveMQ Artemis. Fully superseded; use AMQ Broker.

entries.title: Heketi
entries.description: REST-based volume management for GlusterFS on Kubernetes. Superseded by Rook/Ceph and OpenShift Data Foundation.

entries.title: IBM Blockchain Platform (SaaS)
entries.description: IBM's managed blockchain SaaS on IBM Cloud has been wound down. The underlying Hyperledger Fabric remains open source and active.

entries.title: Watson Discovery
entries.description: AI-powered search and text analytics service. Capabilities moving into watsonx.ai RAG and enterprise search features.

entries.title: Lotus Notes / Domino
entries.description: Collaborative messaging and application platform sold to HCL in 2019. IBM support ended 2024. Avoid for new projects.
